column2: IDC FUNDING DASHBOARD — Empowering South African Entrepreneurs
Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) — Investment Overview Across Fiscal Years

column5: The IDC has historically channeled the largest investments into basic metals, mining, and heavy manufacturing — if your business is in these sectors, the IDC is a natural funding partner.
Agriculture and agro-processing receive consistent funding across all years, making it an accessible sector for entrepreneurs looking for IDC support.
Clothing & textiles companies receive ongoing funding, often through the IDC's subsidiary structures and development fund programs.
From biotech to solar energy to AI — the IDC actively funds innovative, forward-looking ventures under its 'New Industries' mandate.
Media, film, and audio-visual projects have received significant IDC backing — entrepreneurs in the creative economy should take note.
The IDC funds projects across Africa including Mozambique, Lesotho, Rwanda, Congo, and Tanzania — regional entrepreneurs can apply.
From R500K for small businesses to R5.5B+ for major industrial projects — there's funding available at every scale.
Many deals include Worker Trusts and BEE structures — showing IDC's commitment to broad-based economic empowerment.
